auto_generation.note: Future: new game entities auto-generate fragments via LLM at startup

_core: You narrate events in a 2D crafting RPG. The world has 4 material tiers: T1 (common, early-game) through T4 (legendary, end-game). Players fight enemies, gather resources from nodes, craft items across 6 disciplines, and explore a procedurally generated world with forests, caves, quarries, and villages. Damage formula uses multiplicative scaling with stats, equipment, and class bonuses.
_output: Write ONE factual sentence describing this activity. Mention the count and location if regional. Note any notable pattern such as concentrated recent activity, sustained effort, or a new milestone. Do not editorialize or speculate — state what happened.
domain:combat: Combat events. Players fight enemies using melee weapons (sword, axe, dagger, mace, hammer), ranged weapons (bow), and elemental skills. Damage types: physical, fire, ice, lightning, poison, arcane, shadow, holy. Enemies have tiers (T1-T4) and ranks (normal, boss, unique). Status effects include burn, bleed, poison, freeze, stun.
domain:gathering: Gathering events. Players harvest resources from world nodes using tools: pickaxe (ore/stone), axe (wood), hammer (stone). Nodes can be depleted and regenerate. Resources have tiers matching their biome difficulty. Categories: metal ores, wood, stone, plants, fish.
domain:crafting: Crafting events. Six disciplines: smithing (weapons/armor at anvil), alchemy (potions at cauldron), refining (raw materials into processed), engineering (mechanical devices), enchanting (magical properties), fishing. Each has a unique minigame. Quality ranges: normal, fine, superior, masterwork, legendary.
domain:progression: Progression events. Max level 30 with EXP curve 200*1.75^(level-1). Six stats: STR (melee damage), DEF (damage reduction), VIT (health), LCK (crit chance), AGI (speed), INT (magic/mana). Six classes, 40+ titles earned through gameplay milestones.
domain:exploration: Exploration events. World is divided into chunks (16x16 tiles) within localities, districts, and provinces. Biomes include forest, cave, quarry, and water. Dungeons appear with rarity tiers. Danger levels range across regions.
domain:social: Social events. NPCs provide quests and dialogue. Quest types include kill, gather, craft, explore, and talk objectives. Faction reputation affects NPC disposition.
domain:economy: Economy events. Gold earned from quests, loot, and trading. Spent on skill unlocks, repairs, and purchases. Items have rarity tiers affecting value.
domain:skills: Skill events. 30+ skills with mana costs and cooldowns. Categories include combat strikes, elemental spells, gathering buffs, and passive abilities.
domain:items: Item events. Equipment includes weapons (8 types), armor, tools, and consumables. Items have tier, rarity, durability, and weight. Durability at 0%% means 50%% effectiveness, never breaks.
domain:dungeon: Dungeon events. Instanced areas with enemy waves, chests, and a completion objective. Rarity tiers: common, uncommon, rare, epic, legendary.
material_category:metal: Metal ores and ingots. Mined with pickaxes from ore veins. Includes copper, iron, steel, mithril, and higher-tier metals. Primary materials for smithing.
material_category:wood: Wood from trees. Chopped with axes. Includes oak, birch, ash, ironwood, and exotic woods. Used for handles, engineering, and construction.
material_category:stone: Stone and gems. Mined from quarry nodes. Includes limestone, marble, obsidian, and gemstones. Used in refining and engineering.
material_category:elemental: Elemental materials with magical properties. Found in special nodes or dropped by enemies. Used primarily in enchanting and alchemy.
material_category:monster_drop: Materials dropped by defeated enemies. Rarity scales with enemy tier. Used across multiple crafting disciplines.
discipline:smithing: Smithing uses anvil stations to produce weapons and armor from metals. Grid-based placement minigame. Higher tiers require larger grids.
discipline:alchemy: Alchemy uses cauldron stations to produce potions and consumables. Combines reagents with specific properties. Volatile ingredients affect difficulty.
discipline:refining: Refining processes raw materials into usable components. Converts ores to ingots, logs to planks. Foundation for other disciplines.
discipline:engineering: Engineering produces mechanical devices, turrets, traps, and bombs. Requires diverse material types. Most complex recipes.
discipline:enchanting: Enchanting adds magical properties to equipment. 14 enchantment types including damage bonuses, protection, lifesteal, and elemental effects.
discipline:fishing: Fishing uses rods at water nodes. Timing-based minigame. Catches vary by location and tier.
tier:1: Tier 1 is common, early-game content. Routine for experienced players. T1 multiplier: 1.0x.
tier:2: Tier 2 is uncommon, mid-game content. Requires some player investment. T2 multiplier: 2.0x.
tier:3: Tier 3 is rare, late-game content. Significant challenge and reward. T3 multiplier: 4.0x.
tier:4: Tier 4 is legendary, end-game content. Extremely rare encounters and materials. T4 multiplier: 8.0x.
element:physical: Physical damage is the default melee and ranged type. No special effects. Resisted by armor.
element:fire: Fire damage can apply burn (damage over time). Strong against ice enemies.
element:ice: Ice damage can apply chill or freeze (movement/action prevention).
element:lightning: Lightning damage can apply shock. Chain damage potential.
element:poison: Poison damage applies poison DoT. Stacks with multiple applications.
element:arcane: Arcane damage is pure magical energy. Less commonly resisted.
element:shadow: Shadow damage is dark elemental. Associated with stealth and debuffs.
element:holy: Holy damage is light elemental. Effective against undead and shadow creatures.
rank:normal: Normal rank enemies are standard encounters. Common in the world.
rank:boss: Boss rank enemies are rare and powerful. Defeating one is a notable milestone. Higher rewards.
rank:unique: Unique rank enemies are one-of-a-kind encounters. Significant world events.
status_effect:burn: Burn deals fire damage over time.
status_effect:bleed: Bleed deals physical damage over time from wounds.
status_effect:poison: Poison deals damage over time, can stack.
status_effect:chill: Chill slows movement and attack speed.
status_effect:root: Root prevents movement but allows attacks.
status_effect:shock: Shock interrupts and deals burst lightning damage.
status_effect:knockback: Knockback pushes the target away from the source.
status_effect:weaken: Weaken reduces the target damage output.
status_effect:haste: Haste increases movement and attack speed.
status_effect:encumbered: Encumbered from heavy inventory, reduces speed.
species:wolf_grey: Grey Wolf is a tier 1 enemy found in forest biomes. Wolf are pack predators that hunt in groups. Low individual threat.
species:wolf_dire: Dire Wolf is a tier 2 enemy found in forest biomes. Wolf are pack predators that hunt in groups. Moderate threat.
species:wolf_elder: Elder Wolf is a tier 3 enemy found in forest biomes. Wolf are pack predators that hunt in groups. Dangerous opponent.
species:slime_green: Green Slime is a tier 1 enemy found in caves and wet areas. Slime are amorphous creatures that split and reform. Low individual threat.
species:slime_acid: Acid Slime is a tier 2 enemy found in caves and wet areas. Slime are amorphous creatures that split and reform. Moderate threat.
species:slime_crystal: Crystal Slime is a tier 3 enemy found in caves and wet areas. Slime are amorphous creatures that split and reform. Dangerous opponent.
species:beetle_brown: Brown Beetle is a tier 1 enemy found in underground areas. Beetle are heavily armored insects. Low individual threat.
species:beetle_armored: Armored Beetle is a tier 2 enemy found in underground areas. Beetle are heavily armored insects. Moderate threat.
species:beetle_titan: Titan Beetle is a tier 4 enemy found in underground areas. Beetle are heavily armored insects. Extremely dangerous.
species:golem_stone: Stone Golem is a tier 3 enemy found in quarry and cave biomes. Golem are animated stone constructs, slow but powerful. Dangerous opponent.
species:golem_crystal: Crystal Golem is a tier 4 enemy found in quarry and cave biomes. Golem are animated stone constructs, slow but powerful. Extremely dangerous.
species:void_wraith: Void Wraith is a tier 4 enemy found in corrupted end-game areas. Void are ethereal beings from the void. Extremely dangerous.
species:entity_primordial: Primordial Entity is a tier 4 enemy found in deep end-game areas. Entity are ancient primordial beings of immense power. Extremely dangerous.
